title: St. Patrick's Day.
content:
There's an Isle, a green Isle, set in the sea,
         Here's to the Saint that blessed it!
        And here's to the billows wild and free
         That for centuries have caressed it!

        Here's to the day when the men that roam
         Send longing eyes o'er the water!
        Here's to the land that still spells home
         To each loyal son and daughter!

        Here's to old Ireland - fair, I ween,
         With the blue skies stretched above her!
        Here's to her shamrock warm and green,
         And here's to the hearts that love her!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jean Blewett, titled "St. Patrick's Day.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
